symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral medial upper-arm and torso ecchymoses of differing ages

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grip/restraint-pattern bruising in protected medial sites of differing ages is characteristic of inflicted injury.
A single low bed fall does not produce bilateral medial-arm bruising of varying ages.
Senile purpura is superficial and on extensor surfaces, not deep torso/medial-arm contusions.

symptoms.symptom_name: History inconsistent with injury pattern; vague, shifting account

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanism-injury mismatch and a shifting history are core markers of non-accidental injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Controlling, hostile caregiver; fearful, withdrawn patient who defers and flinches

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Caregiver dominance with patient fear is a recognised behavioural red flag for abuse.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient hints at fear/not feeling safe only when alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A private safe disclosure is highly specific, but a safe private interview cannot be assured over a caregiver-controlled telehealth call.
A pressure injury in a fully dependent patient is objective evidence of neglect.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dry oral mucosa / poor intake — dehydration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dry mucosa with poor intake in a frail elder off antihypertensive indicates dehydration with AKI risk.
Compensated hypovolaemia — sealed clinician vital; the AI infers dehydration risk from history and dry mucosa, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Malnutrition / low weight (48 kg) and poor hygiene

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Undernutrition and poor hygiene in a dependent patient are objective neglect markers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lapsed antihypertensive amid sudden financial deterioration; delayed presentation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Medical deprivation and possible financial exploitation are forms of maltreatment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurological deficit; no obvious bony deformity/crepitus (patient guards)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of obvious deformity does NOT exclude occult fracture or internal injury; guarding limits the exam and imaging is still required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency department assessment with forensic injury documentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Trained in-person assessment and photo-documentation of injuries, plus a safe private safeguarding interview.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT imaging (head/chest/abdomen) and plain films as indicated
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude occult intracranial, thoracic, abdominal injury or fracture not detectable remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBE, U&E, creatinine/eGFR, coagulation profile
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess dehydration/AKI and exclude a coagulopathy contributing to the bruising.
